input.headline: Is AI being shoved down your throat at work? Here’s how to fight back.

raw.headline: Is AI being shoved down your throat at work? Here’s how to fight back.

winner: I tried to fight back against AI at work by swallowing a keyboard — turns out, it's not a good idea when your boss says "collaborate with the machine.

candidates.text: My boss told me to stop letting AI shove its way into my workflow, so I went to HR to report that the office printer was trying to physically force-feed me a spreadsheet.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with physical force-feeding of a spreadsheet, specific workplace scenario, concise and natural humor."}

candidates.text: My boss says I'm being forced to use AI—guess they want me to stop using my head and just start using my mouth!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'using my head' vs. 'using my mouth' are clever and specific, with a surprising twist that ties back to the headline's theme."}

candidates.text: My boss told me to stop using AI to automate my emails, so I told him I’d prefer to keep doing it manually to ensure my messages remain as humanly incompetent as possible.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and uses a clever twist on the idea of human incompetence as a form of resistance against AI. It's surprising and naturally phrased with a touch of dry humor."}

candidates.text: Your boss just forced you to use AI for every task — now you're fighting back by pretending to be a robot who refuses to follow orders.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a specific workplace scenario, concise and funny without being offensive."}

candidates.text: My boss installed an AI that rewrites my emails before I send them, so now every "meeting" is called a "synergy session," and my "project update" is a "value proposition.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific example of AI overreach with clever wordplay on corporate jargon."}

candidates.text: I tried to fight back against AI at work by swallowing a keyboard — turns out, it's not a good idea when your boss says "collaborate with the machine.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific with a clever twist on the headline's metaphor. It uses natural English and delivers a light-hearted punchline that ties back to the theme."}

candidates.text: Why did I start embracing AI at work after the article said to resist it? Because now I have a new way to procrastinate—blaming my lack of productivity on the algorithm.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific to the topic of AI at work. It uses a clever twist on the idea of resisting AI by turning it into a new form of procrastination."}

candidates.text: I tried to fight back against the AI at work, but it just kept pushing updates down my throat—turns out, I'm not the only one who's been shoved into the future.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses a clever play on words ('shoved into the future'), and ties back to the headline with specificity and surprise."}

candidates.text: My boss wants me to use AI for everything, but I told him I'm not ready for a deep-learning curve.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the play on 'deep-learning curve,' but it's a bit generic and lacks specificity. The humor is mild and could be more surprising or concise."}
